objective: Deliver a runnable owner-facing long-term-memory vertical slice with SQLite as the authoritative store, Qdrant local mode as a rebuildable derived vector index, explicit candidate-to-consent-to-promotion workflow, provenance/trust/sensitivity/TTL/version fields, scoped retrieval into chat as untrusted data, correction/pin/export/delete operations, deletion receipts after reconciliation, and a dashboard-style Dev Console that groups related real features into understandable navigable pages.
non_goals: Do not auto-promote any chat, model, web, screen, OCR or public input into long-term memory.
Do not treat Qdrant or the lexical embedding as the source of truth or claim the M06 semantic-retrieval quality gate.
Do not put retrieved memory into the system role or allow memory text to override persona or safety policy.
Do not expose memory or Qdrant outside loopback or add a cloud dependency.
Do not claim deletion from already-trained model weights or adapters.
Do not add M07 avatar functionality in this slice.
Do not preserve the old single-page wall of panels; navigation must expose grouped dashboard views without fake data.
contracts: SQLite append-only candidate/event/audit data is authoritative; Qdrant contains only derived vectors and payload metadata and must be fully rebuildable.
Every candidate records source, trust, owner, confidence, sensitivity, TTL, status, content hash, timestamp and version.
No candidate is promoted without an explicit owner decision; public and untrusted sources are never auto-promoted.
Sanitation runs before candidate persistence. Injection-bearing input is quarantined without storing its raw content.
Retrieval revalidates every Qdrant result against active authoritative SQLite rows, owner scope, expiry and sensitivity before use.
Owner memory is retrieved only for owner.console turns and is inserted into a delimited user-role context block labeled untrusted memory data.
Delete redacts active SQLite content, removes the vector, clears candidate/cache state and returns a DeletionReceipt only after reconciliation.
The browser dashboard uses hash routes so refresh and navigation stay inside the loopback app; no feature is represented by canned backend data.
Every dashboard page and functional group includes plain-language purpose and usage notes for non-developers.
allowed_dependencies: Python 3.12+ standard library and existing Hina runtime, contracts, safety and text-brain packages.
qdrant-client 1.18.0 in persistent local mode, pinned in uv.lock and wrapped behind Hina's derived-index interface.
Existing dependency-free HTML, CSS and JavaScript Dev Console; no frontend framework migration in this slice.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover migrations, candidate sanitation/quarantine, explicit promotion, provenance fields, public-source no-auto-promotion, owner isolation, expiry, contradiction-safe retrieval, correction/pin and exact-version conflicts.
Crash-safe tests cover SQLite promotion plus pending Qdrant outbox, idempotent reconciliation, orphan-vector cleanup and full rebuild.
Delete tests prove no receipt is returned before SQLite, Qdrant, candidate queue and derived cache are reconciled.
Conversation tests prove owner memory is added only as a delimited untrusted user-role block and never retrieved for public/viewer/authenticated turns.
Runtime endpoints provide status, candidates, decisions, records, search, correction, pin, export and delete with stable errors and redacted logs.
Dev Console dashboard has header, responsive sidebar/navbar and grouped Overview, Companion, Memory, Safety and Runtime pages.
All existing web controls remain reachable and functional after navigation, while every page and feature group explains its purpose in plain Vietnamese.
Fast unit, contract, governance/provenance, JavaScript syntax and Dev Console startup smoke remain green.
Browser verification on 127.0.0.1 confirms navigation, responsive layout, readable annotations and a real memory candidate-to-promotion-to-search workflow.
required_test_commands: uv run --frozen python -m unittest discover -s tests/governance -p "test_*.py"
